diary:
June 1st felt like one of those steady, sunny Mondays where everything just… keeps moving. I woke up before my alarm, made a strong espresso, and checked the clinic schedule twice—old habit, because mornings can be weird. On the drive into Factoria, the light rail closure between Westlake and University District had folks grumbling in traffic, but I managed to stay ahead of it. The clinic was busy in that familiar way: appointments, meds prep, gentle coaxing for nervous animals. I remember one little recovery patient—wrapped up snug, eyes half-lidded—so calm it almost made me laugh.

By lunch at Whole Foods in Crossroads, I grabbed something quick and stood by the window watching the bright day. Later, after restocking and rounds, I picked up brewing stuff and pantry staples at QFC in Kirkland. Volunteering at the Northshore Senior Center was the right kind of tiring—careful routines, steady hands. I met a friend for dinner at Paseo, then walked the waterfront at Juanita Bay Park, clear sky and all. I ended the night feeling quietly grateful, like I’d earned the calm.
thought_process: I planned my morning around the light rail closure even though I drive, just to avoid any ripple effects—detours, congestion, people rushing. Arriving early felt worth it because the clinic runs smoother when I can prep meds and get the recovery areas settled before the day gets loud. At work I focused on the tasks that reduce stress for everyone: gentle handling, accurate restocks, and keeping routines consistent. The World Cup game being on at 11am didn’t distract me much, but I did notice how the downtown energy shifted—like people were already in “daytime weekend” mode, which made my errands feel a bit lighter. Volunteering later grounded me; I could feel my mood soften with each packed box. By dinner and the waterfront walk, I was tired in a good way, not drained.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
